question_text: Describe the difference between transverse and longitudinal waves in terms of particle movement.
correct_answer: In transverse waves, particles vibrate perpendicular (at right angles) to the direction of wave travel. In longitudinal waves, particles vibrate parallel (in the same direction) as the wave travels.
explanation: This is the key difference between the two wave types. Transverse waves look like up-and-down wiggles (like a rope), while longitudinal waves have compressions and rarefactions moving in the direction of travel (like a spring).
